Fixed Deposit Product Guide
Page 1
Internal Reference – Customer Service
 Fixed Deposit – Product Guide
 Scheme features, rate card, and comparison reference
 Document Code: FD-PROD-02  |  Version 1.0  |  June 2026
 Fixed Deposit Servicing Knowledge Base

Fixed Deposit Product Guide
Page 2
Internal Reference – Customer Service
This guide describes the Fixed Deposit (FD) schemes currently offered, their features, indicative interest
rates, and how they compare against other common deposit and investment options. It is intended to
help customer service teams answer product and comparison queries accurately.
1. Product Overview
Regular Fixed Deposit
A standard term deposit available to individuals, joint holders, and entities, with tenures from 12 to 60
months. Available in both cumulative (interest compounded and paid at maturity) and non-cumulative
(periodic payout) variants.
Senior Citizen Fixed Deposit
Identical structure to the Regular FD, with an additional rate benefit for depositors aged 60 years and
above. Available in cumulative and non-cumulative variants across the same tenure range.
Tax-Saving Fixed Deposit
A 5-year fixed-tenure deposit that qualifies for a tax deduction under the applicable section of the Income
Tax Act, up to the prescribed annual limit. This variant does not permit premature withdrawal or loan
against the deposit, except in case of the depositor's death.
Flexi / Sweep-in Fixed Deposit
A deposit linked to a savings account that automatically sweeps surplus balance above a threshold into an
FD, and sweeps funds back to the savings account if the balance falls short of a transaction. Offers
savings-account liquidity with FD-like returns on the swept amount.
2. Indicative Interest Rate Card
Rates below are indicative and illustrate the general slab structure; the rate applicable to a specific deposit
is the one published on the booking date and locked in for the chosen tenure.
Tenure
Regular (% p.a.)
Senior Citizen (% p.a.)

6.80%
7.05%

7.10%
7.35%
36 months to 44 months
7.40%
7.65%

7.25%
7.50%
Tax-Saving (60 months)
7.25%
7.50%
Note: Senior citizen rates apply to the primary holder only. Bulk deposits (above Rs 2 crore) are priced on a
separate card and are not covered by the rates above.

4. How a Fixed Deposit Compares
This section is a quick reference for the comparison questions customer service teams receive most often.
It is general guidance, not financial advice, and should be presented to depositors as such.
Fixed Deposit vs Recurring Deposit (RD)
An FD requires a lump-sum deposit upfront and is suited to depositors who already have a corpus to
invest. An RD is built through fixed monthly instalments and suits depositors looking to save gradually
toward a future goal. RD interest rates are typically comparable to FD rates of similar tenure, but the
effective yield is lower because not all the money is invested from day one.
Fixed Deposit vs Mutual Funds (Debt or Equity)
An FD offers a fixed, pre-known return and capital protection, with no market-linked fluctuation. Mutual
funds carry market risk and the return is not guaranteed, but they offer the potential for higher returns over
the long term, particularly for equity funds. Depositors prioritising safety and predictability are generally
better suited to an FD, while those comfortable with market risk in exchange for potentially higher
long-term returns may prefer mutual funds. The right mix depends on the depositor's goals, time horizon,
and risk appetite.
Fixed Deposit vs Savings Account
A savings account offers full liquidity but a materially lower interest rate. An FD locks in funds for a chosen
tenure in exchange for a substantially higher rate. A Flexi / Sweep-in FD is often the right middle ground
for depositors who want both liquidity and a better rate on surplus balances.
